senses.definition: an official contract or arrangement by which a bank gives money to someone as a loan to buy a house and the person agrees to repay the loan over a specified period, usually with interest

examples.x: They took out a mortgage to buy their first house in the suburbs.
The monthly mortgage payments include principal and interest, as well as property taxes and insurance.
He refinanced his mortgage to take advantage of lower interest rates and reduce monthly payments.
Failure to make mortgage payments on time can lead to foreclosure, where the lender repossesses the property.

senses.definition: a building or the piece of land surrounding it, owned by individuals, businesses, or entities

examples.x: They inherited a large property with acres of farmland and a historic farmhouse.
Real estate agents help clients buy and sell residential and commercial properties.
He invested in rental properties as a source of passive income.
Property values in the city have been steadily increasing due to high demand and limited supply.
The deed and title documents confirm ownership of the property and its legal boundaries.

senses.definition: a modern building that is very tall, often built in a city

examples.x: The city skyline is dominated by a new skyscraper that towers over all the other buildings.
He works in a skyscraper located in the financial district.
The architect designed a futuristic skyscraper with glass facades.
From the top of the skyscraper, you can see the entire city.
The skyscraper was built to withstand high winds and earthquakes.

senses.definition: a very large and impressive house

examples.x: The billionaire lives in a luxurious mansion overlooking the ocean.
They were invited to a gala event hosted at a historic mansion in the countryside.
The mansion has ten bedrooms, a swimming pool, and a large garden.
The old mansion has been converted into a boutique hotel.
He always dreamed of owning a mansion with a grand staircase and a library.

senses.definition: a building or a group of buildings in which individual units are owned privately, while common areas and facilities such as hallways, elevators, etc. are owned and managed by all residents

examples.x: She bought a condominium in the downtown area for its convenient location and amenities.
The condominium association holds regular meetings to discuss maintenance and community issues.
Residents of the condominium enjoy access to shared facilities such as a swimming pool and fitness center.
Condominiums are popular among urban dwellers seeking homeownership with shared maintenance responsibilities.
The condominium fee covers maintenance costs for common areas and services provided by the homeowners' association.

senses.definition: an apartment on top of a tall building

examples.x: She moved into a penthouse with stunning views of the city skyline.
The penthouse includes a private terrace, a hot tub, and floor-to-ceiling windows.
The penthouse is considered the most exclusive residence in the building.
He threw a lavish party in his penthouse to celebrate his promotion.
They stayed in a penthouse suite during their vacation, enjoying unparalleled luxury.

senses.definition: an apartment with two floors each with its own rooms connected by an internal staircase

examples.x: The lower unit of the duplex has a spacious living room and kitchen, while the upper unit has bedrooms and a study.
Duplexes are popular in urban areas for their efficient use of space and potential rental income.
The duplex layout allows for privacy between the two units, making it ideal for extended families or rental properties.
The owners renovated the duplex to update kitchens and bathrooms in both units, attracting higher-quality tenants.
She enjoys the extra privacy provided by the duplex's two floors.

senses.definition: a set of buildings of the same type and design in the same place

examples.x: The apartment complex offers a variety of floor plans and amenities to cater to different resident needs.
The shopping complex includes stores, restaurants, and entertainment venues all under one roof.
The tech startup moved into a modern complex with glass walls and open spaces, fostering a collaborative work environment.
The resort's beachfront complex includes luxurious villas, a spa, and a private marina for yacht owners.
The university’s research complex is a hub of innovation, where scientists and students collaborate on groundbreaking projects.
The sports complex included stadiums, gyms, and other athletic facilities.

senses.definition: an area where multiple residential buildings, such as houses, townhouses, or apartments, are constructed to create a new community or neighborhood

examples.x: The housing development will feature a variety of architectural styles and amenities to attract families and retirees alike.
The local government invested in infrastructure improvements to support the new housing development.
Developers are working on a sustainable housing development that incorporates green building practices and energy-efficient technologies.
Residents in the new housing development enjoy access to amenities such as a community center, playgrounds, and retail shops within walking distance.

senses.definition: a house that is part of a row of similar houses built side by side

examples.x: They purchased a row house in the historic district known for its charming architecture and community atmosphere.
The row houses along Main Street date back to the early 19th century and showcase intricate brickwork.
Each row house has its own private entrance and backyard, providing a balance of privacy and community living.
The row house development includes shared amenities such as a common courtyard and designated parking spaces.
Many urban dwellers prefer row houses for their compact design and proximity to downtown amenities and public transit.

senses.definition: a boat designed for living in

examples.x: They spent their vacation on a houseboat cruising down the river.
The houseboat was anchored at the marina, offering a unique living experience.
She dreams of retiring on a houseboat and traveling from port to port.
The houseboat has all the amenities of a regular home, including a kitchen and bathroom.
They hosted a party on their houseboat, enjoying the sunset over the water.

senses.definition: an area or room directly under the roof of a house, typically used for storage or as an additional living area

examples.x: She converted the attic into a cozy home office with skylights and built-in shelves.
The attic is accessible via a pull-down ladder in the hallway for storing seasonal decorations and old furniture.
They discovered old family heirlooms stored in boxes in the attic while cleaning out the house.
In older homes, attics were originally used as sleeping quarters before modern heating and cooling systems were introduced.

senses.definition: an area or room in a house or building that is partially or completely below the ground level

examples.x: They converted the basement into a home theater with comfortable seating and a big screen.
The basement often floods during heavy rain, so they installed a sump pump.
He keeps his workout equipment in the basement to have a private gym space.
The basement was dark and cool, perfect for storing old furniture and boxes.
She rents out the basement as a studio apartment to earn extra income.

senses.definition: an underground storage space or room, typically found in a building, used for storing food, wine, or other items that require a cool and dark environment

examples.x: They use the cellar to store homemade preserves and pickles.
The cellar was filled with dusty bottles and old wooden crates.
She found a hidden door in the kitchen that led down to the cellar.
He installed a temperature control system in the cellar to keep his wine collection at the perfect temperature.
The old cellar had thick stone walls that kept it cool even in the summer.

senses.definition: a room in an apartment or house that a baby sleeps in

examples.x: They decorated the nursery with soft pastel colors and stuffed animals.
The nursery is located next to the parents' bedroom for easy access during the night.
She spent months preparing the nursery before the baby arrived.
The nursery has a crib, changing table, and a rocking chair for feeding.
They hired a designer to create a calming and beautiful nursery for their newborn.

senses.definition: an outdoor area with paved floor belonging to a house used for sitting, relaxing or eating in

examples.x: They enjoy dining al fresco on their patio during warm summer evenings.
The patio is furnished with comfortable lounge chairs and a table for outdoor gatherings.
She decorated the patio with potted plants and string lights to create a cozy atmosphere.
The patio overlooks a garden and provides a peaceful retreat from city life.
The new house has a spacious patio where they plan to host barbecues and family gatherings.

senses.definition: the external surface of a building roof

examples.x: They had dinner on the rooftop, enjoying the city's skyline at night.
The rooftop garden provides fresh herbs and vegetables for the family.
The hotel has a rooftop pool with panoramic views of the sea.
He loves to relax on the rooftop with a book and a cup of coffee.
The building’s rooftop is equipped with solar panels to generate electricity.

senses.definition: a bell operated by a button outside a house or apartment that makes a sound when pushed, particularly to inform the inhabitants inside

examples.x: She rang the doorbell and waited for someone to answer.
The doorbell chime echoed through the hallway when the delivery arrived.
The doorbell was broken, so visitors had to knock loudly.
They replaced the old doorbell with a new smart model that sends alerts to their phones.

senses.definition: a small step in front of the main door of a building or house

examples.x: She found a package waiting on her doorstep when she returned home from work.
They sat on the doorstep, enjoying the morning sun and sipping coffee.
He wiped his feet on the doormat before stepping onto the doorstep of their newly renovated house.
The children played hopscotch on the doorstep while waiting for their parents to come home.
The delivery person knocked on the door and left the parcel on the doorstep before leaving.

senses.definition: the area around the door at the entrance to a house, room, etc.

examples.x: She stood in the doorway, waving goodbye as he left for work.
The cat sat quietly in the doorway, watching people pass by.
He paused at the doorway to take one last look at the room before leaving.
They decorated the doorway with flowers and lights for the holiday season.
She peeked around the doorway to see who was in the kitchen.

senses.definition: a system that controls the temperature and humidity in a house, car, etc.

examples.x: They had to call a technician to fix the broken air conditioning unit.
She enjoys the refreshing breeze provided by the air conditioning after a long day outside.
The air conditioning in the car was a lifesaver during the long road trip.

senses.definition: a system that provides a building with warm water and temperature

examples.x: The central heating system kicked in as soon as the temperature dropped outside.
They decided to upgrade to a more efficient central heating system for the winter.
The central heating kept every room of the house evenly warm.
She adjusted the central heating thermostat to maintain a comfortable temperature throughout the day.
The old central heating pipes started to make clanking noises as they warmed up.

senses.definition: a device or alarm that starts beeping if it detects smoke or fire

examples.x: The smoke alarm woke them up during the night, alerting them to a fire in the kitchen.
It's important to test smoke alarms regularly to ensure they are functioning properly.
The landlord installed smoke alarms in every room of the apartment building to comply with safety regulations.
She replaced the batteries in the smoke alarm after it started beeping intermittently.
The smoke alarm system is connected to the building's fire alarm system for immediate response in case of emergencies.

senses.definition: a piece of furniture with drawers used for storing clothes and personal items

examples.x: The antique bureau in the corner of the room showcased intricate craftsmanship and served as a storage solution for vintage clothing.
She organized her socks and undergarments neatly in the bureau's drawers, making it easy to find what she needed each morning.
The family heirloom, a beautifully crafted bureau, had been passed down through generations, preserving memories and clothing from different eras.
The carpenter customized the bureau to have extra-large drawers, accommodating the extensive wardrobe of the fashion-conscious homeowner.

senses.definition: a sliding box-shaped piece of furniture found within a desk, dresser, or cabinet, used for organizing and storing items

examples.x: She keeps her socks and underwear neatly folded in the top drawer of her dresser.
He rummaged through the desk drawer to find a pen and paper to jot down a quick note.
The bottom drawer of the filing cabinet is used for storing old documents and files.

senses.definition: an object for collecting and temporarily storing trash or waste materials, often placed outside a house

examples.x: The garbage can in the park was overflowing with discarded wrappers and bottles.
The city provides residents with recycling bins alongside garbage cans to encourage recycling.
They kept a small garbage can under the desk for disposing of paper scraps and packaging materials.
She emptied the kitchen garbage can into the larger bin outside.

senses.definition: a task, especially a household one, that is done regularly

examples.x: Taking out the trash is one of the daily chores he is responsible for.
She created a chart to divide the household chores among her roommates.
Washing dishes is a chore that no one enjoys, but it has to be done.
The kids are assigned specific chores to help teach them responsibility.
Doing the laundry is a weekly chore that often takes up an entire afternoon.

senses.definition: to clean a surface by wiping it with a handle attached to a sponge or cloth at its end

examples.x: She mops the kitchen floor every evening to keep it clean.
The restaurant staff mops the dining area after closing to prepare for the next day.
The janitor mops the hallway floors to maintain cleanliness.
He mops the bathroom tiles to ensure they are free from dirt and grime.
They mop the garage floor regularly to keep it free from oil stains and dirt.

senses.definition: to clean a surface by rubbing it very hard using a brush, etc.

examples.x: She scrubs the bathtub to remove soap scum and stains.
The maid scrubs the kitchen floor to ensure it's spotless.
He scrubs the outdoor patio furniture to remove dirt and grime.
Before painting, they scrub the walls to remove any dirt or grease.
After a day of gardening, she scrubs her hands to remove soil and stains.

senses.definition: to clean a place by using a broom

examples.x: She sweeps the kitchen floor every evening after dinner.
The janitor sweeps the hallway to keep it clean and tidy.
He sweeps the porch to remove fallen leaves and dirt.
Before opening the store, employees sweep the sidewalks to welcome customers.
After the party, they sweep the living room to pick up crumbs and spilled snacks.

senses.definition: to clean a surface by using a machine that sucks up dirt, dust, etc.

examples.x: She vacuums the carpets in the living room every week to keep them clean.
He vacuums the upholstery on the furniture to remove pet hair.
The housekeeper vacuumed the entire house before guests arrived.
He vacuums the car interior to keep it tidy and free from crumbs.
They vacuum the rugs and mats in the entryway to remove dirt and mud.

senses.definition: to clean or dry a surface using a cloth, etc.

examples.x: She wiped the kitchen counter with a damp cloth to remove crumbs.
After washing the car, he wiped it dry with a soft towel to prevent water spots.
He wiped his hands on a towel after washing them in the sink.
She wiped her glasses with a microfiber cloth to remove fingerprints.
The chef wiped the cutting board clean after chopping vegetables.

senses.definition: to equip a room, house, etc. with furniture

examples.x: They decided to furnish the living room with a comfortable sofa, coffee table, and stylish chairs.
The newlyweds are excited to furnish their first home with a bedroom set and kitchen essentials.
To create a cozy workspace, she plans to furnish her home office with a desk, chair, and bookshelves.
The interior designer helped them furnish the dining area with an elegant table and matching chairs.
The office manager chose to furnish the conference room with a large table, comfortable chairs, and audiovisual equipment.

senses.definition: to put a substance over the surface of something, often as a covering

examples.x: The carpenter decided to coat the wooden furniture with a layer of protective varnish.
To enhance durability, the metal parts were coated with a rust-resistant material.
The chef carefully coated the chicken with a flavorful marinade before grilling.
Before winter, the homeowner chose to coat the exterior walls with weather-resistant paint.
To achieve a glossy finish, the artist decided to coat the artwork with a clear sealant.

senses.definition: a detailed plan or design, typically technical or architectural, that outlines the dimensions, materials, and specifications for construction or production

examples.x: Engineers use blueprints to guide the construction of bridges and highways.
The contractor referred to the blueprint to ensure accurate measurements during construction.
The blueprint included diagrams and annotations for plumbing and electrical systems.
